2: Even from everlasting to everlasting, thou art art God.
3: And sayest, Return, ye children of men.
4: When it is past, and as as a watch in the night.
5: In the morning they are they are like grass which which groweth up.
6: In the evening it is cut down, and withereth.
7: And by thy wrath are we troubled.
8: Our secret sins sins in the light of thy countenance.
9: We spend our years as a tale that is told that is told .
10: Yet is is their strength labour and sorrow; For it is soon cut off, and we fly away.
11: Even according to thy fear, so is so is thy wrath.
12: That we may apply our our hearts unto wisdom.
13: And let it repent thee concerning thy servants.
14: That we may rejoice and be glad all our days.
15: And And the years wherein wherein we have seen evil.
16: And thy glory unto their children.
17: And establish thou the work of our hands upon us; Yea, the work of our hands establish thou it.
